artPrompt: A red ticket dispenser on a beige counter, a single torn paper tab curling from its slot, an endless blank waiting bench receding into hazy ivory corridor beyond, cold cyan light flickering on empty seats, a faint arcane glow drifting from stacked smudged forms, the air thick with the melancholy of a number that will never be called, rendered as soft pastel impressionism — hazy diffuse light, visible chalky texture, gentle gradients, dreamy muted color.
name: Please Take a Number
text: Each opponent skips the draw step of their next turn unless they pay {2}. (Service is by appointment only.)
flavorText: The little paper tickets go up to 9,999. Nobody has ever seen 9,999 called.
